inventoryAvoidance.source: Oracle-reviewed Existing Automation Inventory To Avoid

commonNonDestructiveGuarantees: fixture or dry-run only for mutable-looking labels

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a broad root query, rekey it into a built-in source prefix, arrow to a different visible row, resize the window, then press the actions shortcut. The visible row label, footer Enter label, selected semantic ID, and opened action subject must all describe the same command.
truthAssertions: visible row label matches selected semantic id
footer primary label matches resolved action
actions parent subject matches selected command id

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a query that narrows to one built-in, open actions, press Escape back to root, clear the input, and resize from mini to narrow. The placeholder, empty-state text, footer labels, and selected row must not keep stale text from the prior built-in.
truthAssertions: selected row is current after clear

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I search using an alias that matches multiple built-ins, move selection rapidly, open actions, and inspect the primary action. The displayed alias, canonical command title, route target, and handler ID must agree.
truthAssertions: action subject matches selected semantic id

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I filter root until a recent command and a built-in command with similar names appear adjacent, toggle selection between them, open actions, then close actions. The footer must say whether Enter will run the recent command or open the built-in surface, never a blended label.
truthAssertions: footer intent distinguishes recent from built-in

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a no-result query, append text until a built-in appears, resize twice, and open actions. The no-result copy must disappear, the footer must use the recovered row's command text, and the action dialog must not preserve the no-result disabled state.
truthAssertions: actions dialog has current parent state

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I select a built-in row, move focus out and back to the root input, then use keyboard navigation and open actions. Shortcut glyphs shown in the row, footer, and action dialog must match the active focus owner and actual keyboard route.

As a user, I select a row with a long subtitle, resize so text truncates, then open actions. The visible title/subtitle truncation may shorten text, but the receipt must prove the selected command ID and action subject still match the untruncated row.
truthAssertions: truncated visible label keeps semantic id
action parent subject matches selected command
layout generation does not alter command identity

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type, clear, and retype filters that move a built-in between headings, then open actions on the selected row. Heading counts, selected row metadata, footer action label, and action dialog parent heading must not contradict each other.
truthAssertions: selected row heading matches action parent

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I select a safe but unavailable built-in, such as one requiring a missing passive fixture/provider, open actions, and try Enter. The row label, disabled reason, footer refusal text, and action dialog disabled state must all name the same blocker and refuse activation.
truthAssertions: action dialog disabled state matches row

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a literal query containing source-like punctuation, move selection, resize, and open actions. The UI must truthfully show whether the query is literal search text or source syntax, and must not route to a source command because the text merely resembles one.
truthAssertions: route target follows parsed syntax only

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open actions for a root row, then change the parent filter through a safe direct input path while the action dialog remains open. The action dialog must either keep a clearly captured parent subject or close/rebind; it must not display actions for one row while executing another.
truthAssertions: parent subject is captured or rebound
visible action subject matches handler subject

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open actions, filter to a disabled action, resize the dialog, arrow to an enabled action, then back to disabled. The action row text, disabled reason, footer Enter label, and keyboard refusal must update on every selection.
truthAssertions: keyboard refusal matches selected action state

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I filter actions until section headings and actions interleave, use arrow keys across headings, and press Enter only when an action row is selected. The footer and selected semantic ID must never present a section heading as executable.

As a user, I drill from a parent action into a nested safe sub-action list, resize, then navigate back using Escape and breadcrumb. Breadcrumb text, visible action title, route stack, and handler ID must remain aligned.

As a user, I open actions, focus the actions search field, close actions, and continue typing in the parent filter. The parent input, footer, and selected row must prove focus returned to the parent and did not keep action-search ownership.

As a user, I open actions, resize the popup enough to change row layout, then inspect shortcut glyphs and use one safe shortcut. The visible glyph, keyboard routing receipt, and executed action ID must match.

As a user, I choose a fixture-scoped copy/preview action from actions, see a Copy or equivalent label, and activate it in isolated mode. The UI must truthfully mark it as fixture-scoped or non-system, and receipts must prove no system pasteboard mutation.

As a user, I open actions on a built-in row that has both preview and open-like actions, filter to each, and inspect footer labels. The UI must distinguish preview, reveal, and open actions by actual side-effect class, not by similar row text.
truthAssertions: handler id differs for preview and open-like actions

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open actions, select an action, close with Escape, change selection in the parent, reopen actions, and press Enter. The second action dialog must show and execute only the new parent subject.
truthAssertions: reopened actions use new parent subject
old selected action does not persist

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open actions, type a search that yields no actions, resize, clear the search, and activate a recovered safe action. Empty text, footer disabled state, recovered row label, and action ID must update without stale no-action state.
truthAssertions: footer disabled state clears on recovery
recovered action id matches visible row

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a safe popup from a prompt, then open actions above it, close actions, and continue using the popup. Footer labels must always describe the topmost interactive surface, not the buried parent.
truthAssertions: buried parent does not supply active footer intent

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I tab between the filter input, list row, and footer controls while resizing the window. The footer Enter label must describe the focused/selected target that will actually receive Enter at that moment.
truthAssertions: selected semantic id matches Enter receiver
layout change does not alter target identity

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I move from root to a built-in surface to actions to a nested popup, then press Escape step by step. Escape/help text must truthfully describe whether it will close popup, close actions, return to parent surface, or hide the window.
truthAssertions: route generation advances on each close

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a filter, open actions, resize so the input decoration truncates, then return to the parent. The footer must truthfully expose clear-filter availability only when the parent still has a filter.
truthAssertions: truncated decoration does not erase real query

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I filter a list so the selected row disappears, open actions or press Enter, then clear the filter. Footer labels must refuse primary activation while no executable row is selected and recover only when a valid selection exists.
truthAssertions: footer refuses when selected semantic id is absent
action opening fails closed without valid subject
footer recovers after valid selection returns

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type quickly into the root filter, open actions immediately after the last character, and resize during debounce. The visible input text, filtered row set, selected row, footer, and action parent subject must reflect the same final query generation.
truthAssertions: input text and route generation align
action parent subject matches final selected row

scenarios.userStory: As a user using composition-like staged input, I open actions or inspect footer labels before the text is committed. The UI must distinguish staged text from committed filter text and must not run actions based on uncommitted input.
truthAssertions: staged text is distinct from committed query
actions do not bind to uncommitted text

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I select part of the parent input text, open actions, close actions, and continue typing. The input selection/cursor range, filter text, and footer clear/submit labels must prove the action dialog did not corrupt the parent edit state.

As a user, I type a very long query, resize the window through multiple widths, then open actions on a selected result. Truncated input display must not change the real query, selected result, or action subject.
truthAssertions: input display truncation preserves query value

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I type a query containing characters that also appear in menu/source syntax, open actions, clear part of the input, and re-open actions. The UI must truthfully classify each token as literal text or parsed command syntax and show the matching action set.
truthAssertions: token classification is visible in receipts
literal text does not become command syntax

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open Clipboard History in a fixture-only mode, filter, select a row, open actions, and inspect copy, paste, or insert labels. The labels must match the actual safe fixture side effect and prove no system pasteboard write.
truthAssertions: copy/paste/insert labels match fixture side effect

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open Emoji, filter to an emoji, inspect actions, resize, and cancel. The row text, footer, and actions must truthfully distinguish preview/category navigation from inserting an emoji into a target.

As a user, I open File Search in a local fixture, select a file-like row, open actions, filter between preview/reveal/open actions, and cancel. The UI must not label a preview or reveal as an actual open action, and receipts must prove no external app handoff.

As a user, I open Browser History from fixture data, select redacted and non-redacted rows, open actions, and inspect action labels. If a URL is hidden/redacted or activation is disabled, the visible action labels and disabled reasons must say so.

As a user, I open a fixture-backed window list, select focused/minimized/offscreen rows, and open actions. Action labels must truthfully describe activation refusal or preview-only behavior when no real window activation is allowed.
truthAssertions: disabled reason matches fixture window state

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open an app list in no-launch mode, filter to an app, resize the grid/list, and open actions. Row labels and footer must not promise an app launch when the scenario is preview-only or dry-run.
truthAssertions: action sideEffectClass is not external activation
row label stays aligned with selected app id

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a read-only process list fixture, sort/filter/select, and open actions. Any inspect/details actions must be labeled read-only, and no process-control action can appear in this scenario.
truthAssertions: action labels declare read-only side effect

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open Notes with a sandbox note, edit text, open the command bar/actions, switch selection, then cancel. Action labels must truthfully distinguish save, discard, preview, attach, and open behavior against the dirty-state generation.
truthAssertions: dirty-state generation flows into action labels
cancel leaves note state uncommitted outside sandbox

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open Dictation History fixture rows, select transcripts, inspect actions, and cancel. The UI must truthfully label preview versus insert-to-target behavior, with no microphone/media capture.
truthAssertions: preview and insert labels map to sideEffectClass

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open Settings in sandbox/read-only mode, filter preferences, inspect reset-like actions, and cancel. The visible label must not imply a real config write unless the side-effect class is sandboxed and explicitly shown.

As a user, I navigate root to built-in surface to actions to back to root while changing filters at each layer. Every title, breadcrumb, input, selected row, and footer label must reflect the current route generation.

As a user, I select a row, hide/show the app, resize, then open actions. The visible command label, selected semantic ID, and footer intent after show must either restore correctly or clearly reset; no stale hidden-state action may remain.
truthAssertions: visible intent restores or resets explicitly

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a non-destructive confirmation/prompt popup from actions, cancel it, and press Enter in the parent. The parent footer and state must prove the canceled popup did not leave a pending submit/action behind.
truthAssertions: handler id is parent-selected action only

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I select a row, trigger a fixture list refresh that preserves similar visible text but changes IDs, then open actions. The action dialog must bind to the post-refresh selected ID or refuse stale binding.
truthAssertions: visible label alone is not treated as identity

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I select row index 2, filter so index 2 points to a different item, clear the filter, then open actions. The UI must prove action binding follows stable semantic ID, not recycled row index.
truthAssertions: selected semantic id, not row index, owns action binding

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a local delayed built-in fixture, see loading text, resize, wait for ready rows, and open actions. Loading copy, footer disabled state, ready row label, and action ID must transition consistently.
truthAssertions: ready row clears loading disabled reason

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a fixture surface that starts in error, inspect retry labels, retry safely, then open actions on recovered content. Error text, retry button/action, recovered footer, and recovered action subject must not contradict each other.
truthAssertions: error text and retry action match

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I open a surface with passive permission warnings only, inspect actions, resize, and cancel. Warning text and action labels must not promise to open System Settings or mutate permissions in this non-destructive scenario.
truthAssertions: action labels do not promise OS mutation

scenarios.userStory: As a user with main window and a detached/sibling surface visible, I focus one, open actions, switch focus, and inspect labels. Action dialog target identity, footer focus owner, and visible parent title must agree about which surface owns the action.
truthAssertions: action dialog target identity matches parent surface
footer focus owner matches active surface
visible parent title matches action subject

scenarios.userStory: As a user, I change only visual scale/theme fixture state while a selected row and actions dialog are open. Text measurement and layout may change, but command labels, footer intent, disabled reasons, and handler IDs must remain truthful and unchanged.
truthAssertions: layout generation changes without command identity change
footer intent stays tied to selected semantic id
handler id and disabled reason remain truthful
